vBRIEFInfo.description: Add a size cap, entry cap, LRU eviction, and disk quota to the v1 unified content cache (#883 follow-up). Caps are configurable via DEFT_CACHE_MAX_BYTES / DEFT_CACHE_MAX_ENTRIES with sensible defaults (100 MB / 10,000 entries) sized from the v0.26.0 smoke evidence (320 entries = 3.03 MB on disk, ~10 KB/entry avg). LRU is tracked via meta.json mtime touched on cache:get -- zero schema change, zero migration burden for v0.26.0-era cache trees, no edit to the FROZEN cache-meta.schema.json. cache:put refuses-with-eviction on cap breach, and a new cache:prune --to-cap mode lets operators drain the cache on demand. Eviction events are written as cache:evict records to the existing quarantine-audit.jsonl so operators can trace why an entry vanished. Audit retention/rotation (#948) is explicitly out of scope.

plan.title: feat(cache): size cap + LRU eviction + disk quota (#947)

narratives.Problem: v1 unified cache (#883) ships with no size cap, no LRU eviction, and no disk quota. Bulk-triage workflows against an active backlog will grow .deft-cache/ unboundedly until the operator notices a several-GB folder. Concrete real-scale evidence from docs/smoke-2026-05-07-v0.26.0-rerun.md: 320 issue dirs = 3.03 MB on disk (~10 KB/entry average). Extrapolating, a 50,000-issue repo would consume ~500 MB; a multi-repo deployment can hit single-digit-GB inside a session. The cache becomes an operator footgun before any signal surfaces.
narratives.Overview: Add a configurable cap (default 100 MB total bytes / 10,000 total entries; either trips eviction). Track per-entry last-accessed time via meta.json mtime (touched on cache:get). On cache:put, project the new total; if over cap, evict LRU entries until fitting. If the new put still cannot fit (e.g. raw.json larger than cap, or every entry is the just-written one) raise a structured CacheCapBreachedError -> exit code 3. Add cache:prune --to-cap mode that drains LRU until under cap on demand. Every eviction appends one cache:evict record to the existing quarantine-audit.jsonl so operators can trace why an entry vanished. Cap thresholds via env vars (DEFT_CACHE_MAX_BYTES, DEFT_CACHE_MAX_ENTRIES); 0 disables the corresponding cap. Quota-enforcement logic extracted to scripts/_cache_quota.py to keep scripts/cache.py under the 1000-line MUST limit (mirrors the existing _cache_fetch / _cache_validate split).
narratives.Constraint: DO NOT edit vbrief/schemas/cache-meta.schema.json -- the schema is FROZEN and a v0.26.0-era cache tree must keep validating after this PR. The schema-bump alternative (add a 'last_accessed_at' field to meta.json) was rejected because (a) it forces a write-on-read for every cache:get (meta.json rewrite + schema validation on every access -- expensive at scale), (b) it creates a migration burden for pre-existing cache trees that lack the field, and (c) it requires a schema SemVer bump and coordinated change to _cache_validate.py. The mtime-touch approach is atomic (single os.utime syscall), zero schema change, and degrades gracefully (a cache tree from v0.26.0 still has a valid mtime). DO NOT touch scripts/scm.py, tasks/scm.yml, scripts/triage_*.py, tasks/triage*.yml, skills/deft-directive-refinement/SKILL.md, UPGRADING.md, scripts/quarantine_ext.py, scripts/cache_scanner.py (#949 territory). DO NOT scope-creep into #948 (audit retention / rotation -- separate concern). Single PR, draft only (no merge, no ready). PowerShell 5.1 non-ASCII rule (#798) governs all file edits. Wall-clock cap: 3h. Parallel agent on #884 (different machine) shares only origin master + GraphQL bucket; ghx-first / REST-first for all gh calls.
narratives.Outcome: scripts/_cache_quota.py: new helper module with compute_usage(), enforce_caps(), evict_lru(), and a CacheCapBreachedError. scripts/cache.py: cache_get touches meta.json mtime (LRU signal); cache_put calls enforce_caps() pre-write and refuses with exit-3 if cap can't be honored even after eviction; new cache_prune_to_cap() function; --to-cap flag added to cache:prune CLI. Default caps: 100 MB / 10,000 entries (sized from smoke evidence). Env-var overrides: DEFT_CACHE_MAX_BYTES, DEFT_CACHE_MAX_ENTRIES (0 = disabled). Audit log: new cache:evict event records with {source, key, timestamp, reason, trigger, freed_bytes, last_accessed_at}. tests/test_cache.py + new tests/test_cache_eviction.py + tests/integration/test_cache_quarantine.py: cap-breach -> eviction fires; cap-breach + no-evictable -> exit-3; LRU correctness across reads; idempotent prune --to-cap; eviction audit-log format; env-var overrides; mtime-touch on cache:get. tasks/cache.yml: prune subcommand carries --to-cap forwarded via {{.CLI_ARGS}}. CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] -> ### Added bullet referencing #947 with default-cap rationale. task check is green. Draft PR body cites the smoke-evidence sizing methodology. Closes #947.
narratives.Test: tests/test_cache_eviction.py (new): unit tests for _cache_quota helpers covering compute_usage() summation across entries, evict_lru() deterministic ordering by mtime, refusal-on-impossible-fit, env-var override parsing (DEFT_CACHE_MAX_BYTES, DEFT_CACHE_MAX_ENTRIES, including 0 = disabled), CacheCapBreachedError shape. tests/test_cache.py additions: cache_get touches meta.json mtime; cache_put pre-write enforcement evicts oldest entry; cache_put cap-breach + can't-fit -> CacheCapBreachedError -> CLI exit-3; cache_prune_to_cap is idempotent (second invocation no-ops); audit log carries cache:evict records with full schema; default caps applied when env unset; env vars override defaults. tests/integration/test_cache_quarantine.py: end-to-end fetch-all path triggers eviction when N > entry cap and audit log includes cache:evict events for the evicted entries.
narratives.DesignChoice: MTIME-AS-LRU vs SCHEMA-BUMP: chose mtime. cache_get does a single os.utime(meta_path) syscall (no rewrite, no validation). LRU ordering reads stat().st_mtime for every entry's meta.json. Trade-offs: (a) clock-skew between writes is irrelevant -- relative ordering is what matters and a single host's clock monotonicity is sufficient; (b) a v0.26.0 cache tree's existing entries already have a valid mtime (the original write timestamp) so this is backward-compatible without migration; (c) filesystem mtime granularity (1s on most FS) is sufficient -- two reads in the same second may tie but eviction picks deterministically (sorted (mtime, path)); (d) os.utime() on Windows + POSIX is atomic per-inode. Schema-bump alternative rejected per the Constraint section above.

items.title: scripts/_cache_quota.py: new helper module owning compute_usage(), enforce_caps(), evict_lru(), env-var resolution, CacheCapBreachedError
scripts/cache.py: cache_get touches meta.json mtime (single os.utime syscall) so LRU ordering reflects access recency
scripts/cache.py: cache_put projects new total; calls enforce_caps() pre-write; evicts LRU until fitting; raises CacheCapBreachedError if impossible
scripts/cache.py: new cache_prune_to_cap() function; --to-cap flag added to cache:prune CLI; idempotent on repeat invocation
scripts/cache.py: every eviction appends one cache:evict record to quarantine-audit.jsonl with {source, key, timestamp, reason, trigger, freed_bytes, last_accessed_at}
scripts/_cache_quota.py: DEFT_CACHE_MAX_BYTES (default 100 MB) and DEFT_CACHE_MAX_ENTRIES (default 10000) env vars; 0 disables the corresponding cap
scripts/cache.py: CacheCapBreachedError surfaces as CLI exit-3 (distinct from CacheError exit-1, schema exit-2) with structured stderr
tests/test_cache_eviction.py (new): _cache_quota unit coverage -- compute_usage, evict_lru ordering, refusal-on-impossible-fit, env-var override parsing, CacheCapBreachedError shape
tests/test_cache.py: cache_get mtime touch; cache_put pre-write eviction; cap-breach exit-3 path; idempotent prune --to-cap; audit cache:evict format; env-var overrides
tests/integration/test_cache_quarantine.py: fetch-all triggers eviction when N exceeds entry cap; audit log carries cache:evict events
tasks/cache.yml: cache:prune --to-cap forwarded via {{.CLI_ARGS}} (no sources/generates per #574 conventions)
CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] -> ### Added bullet referencing #947 with sizing rationale (100 MB / 10000 entries; ~10 KB/entry from smoke evidence)
task check passes (validate + lint + test + verify:encoding + verify:branch)

Run skills/deft-directive-review-cycle (cap 3 iterations); greptile-errored -> retry once via @greptileai then halt
